text.title: Chunking Document Structure — Procedure
text.summary: 5-step procedure for Markdown and HTML chunkers.

section.title: Step 1 — Detect file type
section.p: Inspect extension: .md / .mdx → MarkdownChunker. .html / .htm → HTMLChunker. Other → defer to chunking-basics.

section.title: Step 2 — Walk structure
section.p: Markdown: regex-detect `^#{1,6}\s+` headers, build a stack tracking the current path. HTML: BeautifulSoup `select` for `section`, `article`, `div:has(h1,h2,h3)`.

section.title: Step 3 — Propagate header_path
section.p: For every emitted chunk, join the ancestor titles by " > " into `header_path`. Prepend the header_path to the chunk text so the embedding contains it.

section.title: Step 4 — Subdivide oversize blocks
section.p: If a section exceeds max_chunk_size, run the recursive splitter from chunking-basics over its body. Each sub-chunk inherits header_path and gets a 0-indexed `part_index`.

section.title: Step 5 — Emit + dedup near-empty
section.p: Apply r2-min-size-guard: chunks under min_chunk_size get merged with previous chunk under the same header_path, or dropped at section boundary. Emit JSONL records.
